channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok Seeks Supreme Court Intervention in US Ban Dispute - Emergency Appeal Filed
item.description: TikTok seeks emergency intervention from the US Supreme Court to block a federal law that would ban the platform unless its China-based parent company agrees to sell it.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: SoftBank Invests $100 Billion in US Projects Under Trump Presidency
item.description: Japanese tycoon Masayoshi Son and President-elect Donald Trump announce plans for SoftBank Group to invest $100 billion in projects in the United States over four years, aiming to create 100,000 jobs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Uh oh... Zotac just leaked Nvidia’s next-gen launch line-up, including RTX 5090 GPU with 32GB of VRAM
item.description: Zotac accidentally spills the details on Nvidia’s initial RTX 5000 range – but there’s no RTX 5060 to be seen.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: TikTok Under EU Scrutiny Over Election Interference
item.description: European Union regulators are investigating TikTok for allegedly failing to address risks to Romania's presidential election, which has been marred by allegations of electoral violations and Russian meddling.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Nothing indicates 'public safety risk' from drone sightings: Kirby
item.description: National security communications adviser John Kirby reiterated on Monday that there is no indication that the drone sightings pose a risk to public safety.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Sonos had a terrible '24. Here's how it hopes to bring you a happier new year
item.description: There's no easy way to say it: 2024 was not a glowing year for Sonos –but the roadmap for 2025 looks brighter.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The point-and-shoot is back: Panasonic launches new Lumix out of the blue, with iPhone-trouncing 30x optical zoom
item.description: The new Panasonic Lumix ZS99 / TZ99 point-and-shoot updates the ZS80 / TZ80 with USB-C charging and will go on sale in February 2025.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 10 steps to turn a failed project into an opportunity
item.description: What got you here will keep getting you here — unless you stop to figure out why it’s happening in the first place. When a project goes off the rails, it’s tempting to focus on the urgent: fix the immediate issues to get things back on track.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Smarter devops: How to avoid deployment horrors
item.description: Many devops organizations implement advanced CI/CD, infrastructure as code, and other automations to increase deployment frequency. In the State of DevOps Report 2023, 18% of respondents were classified as elite performers for being able to deploy on-demand with change lead times of less than a day.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Harris campaign aide accuses Musk of throttling content before election
item.description: A campaign aide to Vice President Harris accused tech billionaire Elon Musk of throttling content on his social platform X ahead of the 2024 election to benefit President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: How to upgrade to the Hyte Y70 PC case to give your parts more breathing room
item.description: The Hyte Y70 is a stylish PC case that will help PC gamers get the most out of their graphics cards and other high-end parts
item.dc:creator: Gieson Cacho, The Mercury News

item.title: What tech teams need to know about WebAssembly
item.description: WebAssembly, or Wasm, is an open standard that defines a portable binary-code format and corresponding text format for executable programs. Created and maintained by the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C), Wasm is designed to facilitate high-performance applications on web pages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why Thrive Capital’s Vince Hankes is betting big on OpenAI
item.description: “We’re in this massive paradigm shift,” says Vince Hankes, the venture capitalist leading Thrive Capital’s investment in OpenAI. “And when that happens, historically, there have been new companies created that are worth a lot of money.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: US agencies should use advanced technology to identify mysterious drones, Schumer says
item.description: U.S. Sen. Chuck Schumer is urging the federal government to deploy advanced drone-tracking technology following weeks of reported drone sightings in parts of New York and New Jersey that have stirred fear and bewilderment.
item.dc:creator: Bianca Vázquez Toness, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Some software stocks may be even less desirable in a rebounding market
item.description: Some recent developments suggest the beleaguered software group of stocks may be coming back into favor, as I noted a couple weeks ago following positive responses to both Snowflake and Elastic.
item.dc:creator: Tiernan Ray, Fast Company

item.title: Apple just brought ChatGPT to iPhones, iPads, and Macs. Here’s how to make the most of it
item.description: This week, Apple rolled out its first significant update to its Apple Intelligence platform since it was launched in October.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Your phone got hacked and now someone's got control of it. What to do immediately
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson lays out the immediate steps you should take if your phone has been hacked and your personal information becomes vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal appeals court blocks push to delay January TikTok ban
item.description: A federal appeals court in Washington denied a request from the social platform TikTok that would effectively delay the ban of the app next month. The rejection comes after the company sought to stop the ban Monday, asking the U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Court denies TikTok's request to halt enforcement of potential US ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday left in place a mid-January deadline in a federal law requiring TikTok to be sold or face a ban in the United States, rejecting a request made by the company to halt enforcement until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge of the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Warning: This fake PayPal email scam is everywhere right now
item.description: Fraudsters are sending people bogus invoices through PayPal as part of a sneaky scam that is going around; here's how to protect yourself from being fooled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Google NotebookLM AI Assistant Now Available to Workspace Customers
item.description: Google's premium version of its NotebookLM AI assistant is now available to Google Workspace customers, offering enhanced features and enterprise-grade protections.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio, Dragon Age, Astro Bot and an indie wave lead the top AP video games of 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s been a rough year in the world of video games, which reeled from some high-profile flops (sorry, Suicide Squad and Star Wars fans) and a relentless series of developer layoffs. Fortunately, indie designers stepped up to fill in the gaps.
item.dc:creator: LOU KESTEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash will require its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing. DoorDash has been under pressure to ensure its drivers are operating legally.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: AMD data center chips vulnerable to revealing data through ‘BadRAM’ attack
item.description: AMD’s Secure Encrypted Virtualization (SEV), meant to protect processor memory from prying eyes in virtual machine (VM) environments, can be tricked into giving access to its encrypted memory contents using a test rig costing less than $10, researchers have revealed.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: From surveillance to safety: How Kazakhstan’s Carpet CCTV is reshaping security
item.description: In a world where technology increasingly shapes how cities manage safety and security, Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Internal Affairs is leading the way with its groundbreaking “Carpet CCTV” project.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google claims breakthrough with Willow quantum computing chip but no real-world use yet
item.description: Google’s announcement on Monday of its new quantum chip, called Willow, was full of eye-catching statements. For starters, Willow took less than a minute to perform a benchmark computation that would take one of today’s fastest supercomputers 10 septillion years to do.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A hardwired TSA looks to the future with 5G implementation
item.description: At TSA most of their critical network infrastructure is hardwired. That includes checkpoint equipment and computers operated by the TSOs. The post A hardwired TSA looks to the future with 5G implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Nokia expands data center SONiC support
item.description: Nokia is offering customers an alternative way to set up data center networks by expanding its data center fabric package to support the open-source Software for Open Networking in the Cloud (SONiC).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Boosting Cyprus' defense is key so close to the war-wracked Mideast, defense minister says
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Bolstering Cyprus ' defense capacity is critical for the east Mediterranean island nation so close to the war-wrecked Middle East, the country's defense minister said Tuesday. The priority for Cyprus is to procure better defense systems, said Defense Minister Vasilis Palmas.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, Fox 5
